Now that we have gone over labels and mirrors, we're going to practice. Here's the scenario. You have a flight to deliver a presentation in New York. You're flying from Denver on Thursday morning and connecting through Chicago. If you don't arrive by Friday afternoon, then you don't get to give your presentation. You show up at the airport and as you're waiting, you hear over the airport's PA system that all flights will be delayed six hours due to a free geistorm. All the runways and planes have to be defrosted before travel can resume as normal. To make matters worse, after checking with your airline, you find out your connecting flight from Chicago to New York got automatically rebooked for an arrival too late for the presentation. You decide to roll the dice anyway and after catching your initial flight to Chicago, you arrive there at 7.30 p.m. You discover there's still a flight to New York. They get you there on time, but it leaves in 25 minutes. You get to the gate and notice that the agent is frustrated because she's beginning to yell that by angry customers. So your goal is to get on this flight or at worst, get an earlier flight the next day. You approach the agent after overhearing a frustrated businessman yelling at her. What do you say to the agent?
